title: Pick Your Perfect Name Day!
story: What if you could wake up and choose any name you wanted? On April 9, National Name Yourself Day lets people do exactly that! Some folks try out nicknames they've always loved, while others experiment with completely different names for fun. Teachers might let students pick classroom names for the day, and families often play games where everyone gets a silly new identity. Social media fills up with people announcing their chosen names and explaining why they picked them. It's a chance to explore who you might be with a different name, or just have fun pretending to be someone new for 24 hours!
activity_question: What do some teachers let students do on National Name Yourself Day?
activity_choices: Pick classroom names for the day
Write their names backwards on everything
activity_answer: Pick classroom names for the day
follow_up_question: If you could pick any name for yourself, what would it be and why?

title: Crunch Time for Chinese Almond Cookies!
story: Every April 9, cookie lovers across America celebrate National Chinese Almond Cookie Day by biting into these golden, crumbly treats! Chinese almond cookies are round, flat cookies with a whole almond pressed right in the center. People visit Chinese bakeries, order takeout from their favorite restaurants, or try baking their own batch at home. The cookies have a rich, buttery flavor and crumble perfectly in your mouth. Many families share them as dessert after Chinese meals or pack them in lunchboxes for a sweet surprise.
activity_question: What makes Chinese almond cookies easy to recognize?
activity_choices: They have a whole almond pressed in the center
activity_answer: They have a whole almond pressed in the center
bonus_fact: These cookies are often served at Chinese New Year celebrations for good luck!
